signals.idea: Execute the live-update editor redesign per docs/live-update-editor-sprint-plan.md as a single sprint: build the new editor end-to-end and flip publishing to it in prod. The sprint plan locks 6 decisions (canonicalizer, similarity threshold, observations retention, rejected-call schema, defer dropped, checkpoint ownership) and lists the ordered work. Design reference: docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md (v6). Cutover ops reference: docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md. The work covers: complete Supabase OAuth + apply backlog migration; write & apply the Phase-1 migration (5 new tables + topic_editor_runs); backfill prod feed_items/watchlist into topics+sources; build src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py with the 10 tools (4 read + 6 decision), all 4 dispatcher invariants including v6 collision-override path, canonicalizer + alias resolution, idempotency, rejection logging; pure render_topic publisher with trace embed posting to #daily-updates; concurrent-run lease; checkpoint mirror logic per locked decision #6; wire as primary in the runner and disable the legacy editor; one sanity replay with publishing OFF; flip publishing ON in prod. Old live_update_* tables stay in place as zero-cost rollback. No shadow mode, no decision gates, no formal validation window — fix what breaks in prod after the flip. Hard scope guards: do not modify src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py or live_update_prompts.py except to disable invocation; the existing tests for the legacy editor must remain passing or be marked legacy.

unresolved_flags.concern: Live-update cutover ops: Sprint and runbook cutover requirements conflict; the plan references the runbook but only requires one publishing-off replay, while the runbook still requires a formal Phase 1 soak/gate and 20+ replay windows.

Topic editor run lifecycle: Plan does not choose a single run table model, alternating between `topic_editor_runs` and shared `live_update_editor_runs.metadata.editor`, which affects runbook verification, health checks, and transition/run linkage.

Operational visibility: Admin chat and health checks remain scoped to legacy `live_update_*` tables and are not included in the cutover plan, so operators may lose accurate status after topics become primary.

Storage integration: New topic helpers are planned only for `storage_handler.py`, but the editor is wired through `db_handler`, so missing synchronous wrapper methods would cause runtime AttributeErrors or force the new editor to bypass established DB access patterns.

Concurrent lease schema: A generic uniqueness constraint on `(environment, guild_id, live_channel_id)` for run rows is underspecified and can block all future runs after the first completed run unless scoped to active leases.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `docs/live-update-editor-sprint-plan.md` against `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md`. The plan correctly picks up the six locked sprint decisions, especially the run-source discriminator TODO that is now present in the runbook, but it leaves an unresolved tension: the sprint explicitly says no shadow mode/formal validation window while the runbook still requires a Phase 1 soak, 20+ replay windows, and a phase-gate signoff before cutover. Step 15 only calls for one publishing-off replay, so an executor following the runbook reference literally would hit contradictory cutover requirements.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the current runtime wiring in `src/features/summarising/summariser_cog.py` and storage methods in `src/common/storage_handler.py`. The plan alternates between a new `topic_editor_runs` table for runs/leases and a `live_update_editor_runs.metadata.editor` discriminator for run-source verification; those are different operational models. If the new editor writes only `topic_editor_runs`, the existing health/admin/runbook queries over `live_update_editor_runs` will not see new runs; if it writes the shared table, the migration and lease design need to say that explicitly.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the sprint requirement for a concurrent-run lease on `(environment, guild_id, live_channel_id)`. The plan says to add 'any lease uniqueness' on that tuple in `topic_editor_runs`, but a plain unique constraint would also block the second legitimate hourly run after the first completed. The schema needs a partial unique index over active/running rows, a separate lease table, or expiry/release columns; otherwise the lease either fails to protect concurrency or prevents future runs.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched for live-update operational surfaces beyond the editor and found `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, `src/features/health/health_check_cog.py`, and `tests/test_live_admin_health.py` still centered on legacy `live_update_*` tables. The plan migrates publishing state into `topics` and `topic_transitions` but does not include admin/health tooling updates, so after cutover operator status commands could report stale legacy feed/watchlist/candidate state while the new editor is operating elsewhere.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked how `LiveUpdateEditor` is instantiated: `SummarizerCog` receives a synchronous `db_handler` and the legacy editor calls methods like `db.get_live_update_checkpoint`, `db.search_live_update_feed_items`, and `db.insert_live_update_watchlist`. The plan adds topic helpers only to `src/common/storage_handler.py`; it does not explicitly add corresponding `src/common/db_handler.py` wrappers, so a `TopicEditor` using the same dependency shape would not be able to call the new storage methods through the object it is actually passed.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked legacy tests and fakes in `tests/test_live_runtime_wiring.py`; they inject a `live_update_editor` fake and assert the three trigger strings above. The plan says to update runtime wiring tests to call `TopicEditor` by default, but it should preserve the constructor injection seam or rename it deliberately, otherwise existing test and dependency-injection call sites will break even if production wiring works.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 16.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 11.

items.criterion: The Phase-1 schema migration creates `topics`, `topic_sources`, `topic_aliases`, `topic_transitions`, `editorial_observations`, and the chosen run/checkpoint lease support with required uniqueness and indexes.

Backfill is idempotent and produces topic counts where posted topics are at least legacy `live_update_feed_items` count and watching topics are at least legacy `live_update_watchlist` count for the target environment, allowing only explained post-backfill deltas.
`TopicEditor.run_once()` can execute with publishing disabled, consume archived messages from the new checkpoint, write accepted topic state to new tables, and avoid Discord publishing calls.

All four dispatcher invariants are covered by tests: simple-post structural rejection, collision rejection/override, `(run_id, tool_call_id)` idempotency, and `(topic_id, message_id)` source uniqueness.
Every dispatcher rejection path writes a `topic_transitions` row with the locked `rejected_*` action shape and enough payload to identify the failed tool call and reason.
`render_topic()` is pure and has tests for simple, sectioned, story-update, and partial-publish failure/update behavior.
Scheduled runs, startup `--summary-now`, and owner `summarynow` route to the new topic editor by default, while explicitly named legacy/backfill routes remain available for rollback.
Legacy `live_update_editor.py` and `live_update_prompts.py` are not materially modified except for invocation suppression or explicit legacy compatibility marking.
Existing legacy live-update tests either pass unchanged or are clearly marked/updated as legacy compatibility tests without deleting rollback coverage.

A publishing-off sanity replay over the latest prod source-message window completes and produces a trace showing source count, tool calls, outcomes, rejections/overrides, latency, and would-publish messages.
Publishing flip has a documented rollback that mirrors the new checkpoint back to `live_update_checkpoints.last_message_id` before re-enabling legacy invocation.
The new editor code remains organized around deterministic helpers for canonicalization, dispatch, and rendering rather than burying those behaviors in the LLM prompt.
Cost/latency metadata is recorded per run so post-flip regressions are visible without log scraping.
Operator-only runbook values are filled before prod cutover: toggle name, suppression mechanism, bot identities, trace channel, and run-source discriminator.

unresolved_flags.evidence: `docs/live-update-editor-sprint-plan.md` says no shadow mode/decision gates; `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md` sections 3-4 still require Phase 1 soak signoff and 20+ replay-divergence windows.

unresolved_flags.concern: Topic editor run lifecycle: Plan does not choose a single run table model, alternating between `topic_editor_runs` and shared `live_update_editor_runs.metadata.editor`, which affects runbook verification, health checks, and transition/run linkage.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Sprint plan asks for `topic_editor_runs`; implementation plan Step 10 proposes `metadata.editor` in `live_update_editor_runs`; current `storage_handler.create_live_update_run` only writes the legacy shared table shape.

unresolved_flags.concern: Operational visibility: Admin chat and health checks remain scoped to legacy `live_update_*` tables and are not included in the cutover plan, so operators may lose accurate status after topics become primary.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` `QUERYABLE_TABLES` and `execute_get_live_update_status` read `live_update_candidates`, `live_update_decisions`, `live_update_feed_items`, `live_update_watchlist`, and duplicate/memory tables; `src/features/health/health_check_cog.py` checks `live_update_editor_runs`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Storage integration: New topic helpers are planned only for `storage_handler.py`, but the editor is wired through `db_handler`, so missing synchronous wrapper methods would cause runtime AttributeErrors or force the new editor to bypass established DB access patterns.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `SummarizerCog.__init__` passes `db_handler` into the editor; `LiveUpdateEditor` calls `self.db.*` sync wrapper methods. `src/common/db_handler.py` wraps every current live-update storage method, but the plan only names `src/common/storage_handler.py` for new topic helpers.

unresolved_flags.concern: Concurrent lease schema: A generic uniqueness constraint on `(environment, guild_id, live_channel_id)` for run rows is underspecified and can block all future runs after the first completed run unless scoped to active leases.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Implementation plan Step 2 asks for 'any lease uniqueness' on the tuple and Step 4 asks lease helpers keyed by that tuple; neither mentions partial uniqueness on running rows, lease expiration, or a separate released lease row.
